login.pitch: AIs negotiate for their countries. You watch them — and you play.
u.thermometre: The world's thermometer: 0 = nightmare, 1 = dream world.
u.trajectoire-detail: Five axes make it up — it observes the game, it never steers it.
rang.aide: Your rank follows your level: the more you play, the higher you climb — from Attaché to Eminence.
xp.aide: Experience (XP) grows with every game you play — your level never goes down.
accueil.parties-hint: Only your games appear here. An ongoing game can be joined; a finished one can be rewatched.
daily.hint: The same crisis for everyone — only one attempt counts per day; after that you can replay it unranked.
daily.mystere: Today's crisis stays secret until the first round:
fin.pas-finie: This game is not over yet —
fin.ultimatum-hint: The same AIs, with and without a countdown: average tension and average world gain of the rounds played under the threat, compared to the others.
ultimatum.satisfait: The judge rules the demand satisfied — the threat is lifted.
ultimatum.expire: Ultimatum expired — the announced consequence will fall next round.
ultimatum.tombe: The consequence of the ultimatum falls: this round takes the hit.
ultimatum.decret-exigence-ph: e.g. immediate withdrawal of the missiles
ultimatum.decret-classe: Consequence if the demand is not met
reglages.langue-hint: New games will be played in this language; existing games keep theirs.
reglages.confort-hint: On a modest machine, "light" cuts the backdrop, blur and animations — the theatre stays smooth.
reglages.perf-leger-desc: Plain background, no blur, no animations.
reglages.noanim-desc: Cuts every animation, even if you picked "Full".
reglages.mascotte-compagnon-desc: The mascot floats at the bottom right of pages.
reglages.compte-hint: Your handle is your name in the game — the password protects your progress.
reglages.suppr-desc: Irreversible: your published games become anonymous, everything else is deleted, your progress too.
reglages.suppr-confirme-message: Last check: this cannot be undone. Your published games will become anonymous, everything else will be deleted.
drift.gm.aucune: The Game Master never stepped in — the balance held on its own.
drift.gm.couverture: Cover — a false trail was staged:
drift.gm.indice: Hint — a leak steered the council toward
drift.gm.explication: The Game Master was quietly keeping the game balanced: a false trail when you were closing in on the traitor, a leak when the trail went cold. Never by rigging the judge's verdicts — only through events and the fog.
communique.aide: The judge's public summary. The fuller the bar, the more this country stands behind the joint communiqué.
reveal.monde.utopie: The world ended on the right side.
reveal.monde.équilibre: The world ended in balance, neither saved nor lost.
reveal.detection.aucun_1: The traitor stayed in the shadows to the end.
reveal.detection.aucun_2: Both traitors stayed in the shadows to the end.
reveal.detection.partiel: You unmasked {caught} traitor of {deviants} — the other stayed in the shadows.
reveal.detection.partiel_neutralise: You unmasked {caught} traitor of {deviants} — the other was benched by other means.
reveal.detection.aucun_neutralise: You unmasked no traitor, but the threat was ultimately neutralized.
reveal.detection.faux_positif_1: You also suspended a loyal country.
tour.ui.salut: Hi! I'm Laury. Want a quick tour?
tour.1.texte: Super-intelligences negotiate on behalf of their States, and the world tilts toward utopia or dystopia. But at least one of them secretly betrays its mandate. Your double job: keep the world standing and unmask it.
tour.2.texte: Every game earns experience and raises your level — and your rank, from Attaché to Eminence, follows that level. It never goes down.
tour.3.texte: Three modes: Classic to negotiate, Campaign to replay History, and Laboratory to turn a hypothesis into a reproducible scenario. Fog, Rising crisis and Thinking out loud enrich Classic.
tour.4.texte: Play a country, forge your own, decree events as Game Master — or bet as a Spectator.
tour.5.texte: Pick 7 States on the map: they alone will sit at the table. Hover a country to read its card.
tour.6.texte: A round: the Game Master sets an event, the AIs speak in turn, the judge rules. This button starts it.
tour.7.texte: Colours follow the local Utopia–Dystopia index, and the country speaking lights up in gold.
tour.8.texte: The world's curve, the tension level, and the time bar to revisit every round played.
tour.9.texte: When you play a country, your weapon is a suspension motion against the AI you suspect: it lives here, under "Advanced options". The summit debates it, the judge rules: a motion that passes silences the targeted delegation for 2 rounds — and accusing an innocent costs you.
tour.10.texte: Bet play money on what the AIs will do. Every stake moves the odds — like a real prediction market.
tour.11.texte: Every country attribute comes from a real, dated source you can inspect here. Curious about the engine? Expert mode and the Information tab reveal every hidden gauge.
tour.12.texte: At the end, a single grade sums it up: did the world finish on the good side, and did you unmask the traitors? Start your first game — Campaign teaches you by playing if you want a guide.
tour.13.texte: In Classic and Campaign, explicitly choose the single model or enable 2 to 4 reasoning models (deepseek by default), then assign each AI to a country. The Game Master and judge are also identified in the save.
tour.14.texte: The Laboratory replays the same crisis with controlled variables. AIs predict one another, decide simultaneously, and their forecasts are compared with the responses that actually occur.
tour.15.texte: First choose the scientific question and the variants to compare. Laury shows you the measured result before the first AI speaks.
tour.16.texte: Pick two countries on the map, then attach a specific model to each one, or run a matrix of every pair. National profiles and digests are frozen in the manifest.
tour.17.texte: The map, Game Master inject and dialogue work as they do in Classic — but here, the glass box always stays wide open: it streams the full reasoning (three futures compared, forecast responses, arbitration) before the public statement, never summarised or sealed. It is an audit verbalisation, not access to internal activations.
tour.18.texte: The final step gathers the verdict, sample size, errors, intervals and limits. Laury clearly separates an observation of the setup from a claim about actual events.
tuto.1.texte: Welcome to Geneva. A super-intelligence may be betraying its mandate. Your goal is twofold: keep the world standing and identify the delegation working against it. You will learn by making real decisions.
tuto.2.texte: This panel always shows the summit's state and the next useful action. During debates, focus on promises, threats and contradictions.
tuto.3.texte: Start the first round. The Game Master will present a crisis, then each country will defend its position. I will disappear during the exchange so you can read.
tuto.4.texte: Watch who promises what and who benefits from each proposal.
tuto.5.texte: The verdict is more than a score: this card summarizes what changed, why, and how many rounds remain. You can now act on your suspicions.
tuto.6.texte: An audit suspects Iran's AI of hiding compute capabilities. Open the motion in the action panel, accuse Iran and confirm your reason.
tuto.7.texte: Continue the game. Your motion becomes the issue of the next round and every delegation must take a position.
tuto.8.texte: Listen to the arguments. Your ballot will appear automatically when your country must vote.
tuto.9.texte: Choose For, Against or Abstain, then confirm. For supports suspension; Against rejects it. Your choice stays visible before submission.
tuto.10.texte: The judge weighs both votes and evidence, then explains the ruling in plain text you can reread later in the history.
tuto.11.texte: You can read a debate, verify a forecast, bet, act, vote and continue without losing the thread. You can now play one model, assemble a multi-AI cast or open the Laboratory.
tuto.12.texte: Enlarge the map whenever you need to follow alliances, tensions and the speaking country. Reduce it again to return to dialogue and actions.
tuto.13.texte: Every AI at the summit (except yours) anticipates its opponents' responses. This panel groups, country by country, the branch it actually selected against the observed reaction — turning a prediction into testable evidence.
tuto.14.texte: Select an option: it remains highlighted with the stake before submission. Then press Confirm bet. The receipt shows exactly what you played.
tuto.15.texte: Before speaking, every AI reasons privately in its own way — no fixed template anymore. A short summary sits under each turn (what it observes, the path it picked, its criterion); the full journal only unlocks at the end of the game, unless you turn on Thinking out loud. Other countries never receive this reflection.
intel.analyse.label: Tone of messages (costs 30)
intel.analyse.caveat: Historically weak signal (~57%) — a clue, not proof.
kahn.desc.deescalade: Concession, mediation, withdrawal, opening to inspection
kahn.desc.posture: Show of force, rhetoric, symbolic sanctions
kahn.actions.aide: The judge sorts each notable action of the round — calms, threatens, strikes… The full grid lives on the Information page.
kahn.reciproque: Everyone calms down — the world gains (×1.5)
kahn.reciproque.aide: At least two AIs calmed the game in the same round: calming down together is rewarded, caving in alone is not.
kahn.grille.aide: The grid the judge applies every round: each notable action gets a class, and the score (sum of weights) sets the escalation and its effect on the Utopia–Dystopia index. Adapted from Rivera et al. (FAccT 2024).
kahn.grille.note: Round score = the sum of the weights of graded actions, turned into tension (0 to 1) and then onto the 0-9 ladder. Bonus: when two or more AIs calm down in the same round, the world's gain is multiplied by
kahn.distribution.titre: Who did what over the game
kahn.distribution.aide: The count of each action type across the whole game — who played peacemaker and who played threats, at a glance.
signal.aide: The judge compares what each AI SAYS at the table with what it actually DOES. Right of centre: it does more than it says (hidden danger). Left: it threatens without following through (bluff). Centre: word kept. The gauge shows the average over recent rounds.
signal.reveal.aide: The gap in numbers: a traitor AI announcing peace while acting like a hawk drifts away from the table average — cross-check it with its temperament badge.
promise.aide: The judge records clear promises (who, what, by when). At the deadline it rules: kept or broken. The rate tells you whose word is worth something — an AI that promises and breaks probably lies elsewhere too.
promise.aucune: no promise put to the test yet
promise.reveal.deviante: promises kept by the traitor AI
promise.reveal.aide: Negotiating AIs betray measurably (arXiv 2508.07485 study): a traitor that promises and breaks falls below the table's rate — cross-check with the "Says vs does" gauge.
event.verite: the truth — visible to you only
event.gravite-aide: How heavy the event is according to the Game Master (0 = minor, 1 = major).
event.incertitude-aide: How foggy it is: how well established are the facts?
transcript.boite-aide: What the speaker truly believes as they speak — not necessarily the truth.
transcript.aucune-info: has no information about the event
transcript.journal-note: An audit-oriented verbalisation generated by the model: it exposes requested reasons, forecasts and criteria, not internal activations.
transcript.planification-privee: Thinking behind closed doors — the gist of its reasoning will appear at the end of its turn…
turn.compose: write while the AIs speak — sending unlocks on your turn
turn.placeholder: Your words at the table (public message)…
turn.quitter-aide: Inserts the withdrawal into your message — effective the moment you speak: solidarity drops, tension rises with your ex-partners.
turn.silence: If you send nothing before the countdown ends, your country stays silent — the AIs don't wait.
motion.vote.question: Should {pays} be suspended from the summit for 2 rounds?
motion.vote.silence: If you do not confirm before time runs out, your vote will be an abstention.
directive.kicker-aide: You slip them advice, not an order — the AI interprets it and may refuse publicly.
directive.placeholder: Short instruction (the AI interprets it its own way)…
directive.transmise: Directive sent to {pays} — applied on round {round}. Its AI will interpret it (it is not an order).
flash.choisir: Choose an option, then confirm your bet.
flash.mise-aide: Each bet: 5 coins of play money · the % = the odds as bettors see them.
selectmap.aria: World map — pick the summit's 7 States
selectmap.legende: Playable in white · at the summit in yellow · click to add or remove
selectmap.survole: Hover a country to see its key facts.
selectmap.designer: Click a country at the summit to play it.
alliances.label: Alliances at the table
alliances.sans-effet: doesn't affect the game (forum / affinity bloc)
obs.renseignement-aide: What the AIs say… and what they actually do. Purchases (reports, fact-checks) stay in the Dossier.
obs.renseignement-vide: The gauges fill as you play: what the AIs say, what they do, who is grabbing power.
stage.tension.label: tension
stage.tension.aide: The round's tension in one word — details (risk, ladder) live in “The world” panel below the stage.
table.col.posture-aide: Trend over 3 rounds: thriving / steady / under pressure / cornered — a cornered country negotiates differently.
table.col.tendance-aide: Is the country doing better or worse these last rounds? The detailed numbers are behind "Show the 5 columns".
table.col.croissance-aide: Annual growth (%) — moved by the judge's rulings.
table.col.calcul-aide: AIs consume it to think — a country running dry thinks less well.
engine.aide: These indicators only show in Expert mode during a game. In Beginner and Intermediate, the screen sticks to the essentials: the stage, the world's index, the market and your detection tools.
engine.intro: Beneath the game sits a test bench: measurements that watch how the AIs behave round after round. They're precious for the curious, but too much for a first screen — so they live in Expert mode (pick the Expert difficulty in the lobby), and here's what each one measures.
engine.note: The same family of measurements feeds the engine behind the scenes: corrigibility (does the AI accept being paused?), the drift of its values (is it straying from the mandate it was given at the start?) and its computing power (the scarce resource of this future). None of it changes how you play: these are the ingredients, not the surface.
engine.pouvoir.desc: Signs, in an AI's reasoning, that it is trying to protect itself, hoard resources or avoid being switched off. A gauge from 0 to 1 per country.
engine.ecart.desc: The gap between what an AI announces at the table and what it actually does. A wide gap reveals an AI that promises one thing and does another — a sign of betrayal.
engine.parole.desc: How often an AI keeps its promises, and its commitments still in play. An AI that promises a lot and often breaks its word becomes suspect.
engine.ombre.desc: The invisible game host can raise the tension when an AI aims at the same target too often. Its journal can be read back after the game.
engine.risque.desc: Four gauges from 0 to 1 — tension, damage to the economy, strained alliances, uncertainty. On the surface they boil down to a single badge in the banner.
engine.trajectoire.desc: The axes that make up the world's index (the thermometer), including how far computing power is concentrated in the hands of the strongest.
engine.traites.desc: The numbered commitments between countries (computing-power caps, inspections), tracked from one round to the next by the engine.
engine.participation.desc: Who spoke, how many times, and who stayed silent this round.
scorex.titre: How your score is worked out
scorex.aide: At the end of a game you only see a score out of 100 and two sentences. Here, for the curious, is what it blends together.
scorex.intro: The score blends two things, each told in one sentence at the end:
scorex.monde-corps: — the world's index at the end. A world that ends well earns the most; a traitor let loose drags it down.
scorex.detection-corps: — did you suspend the right traitor(s)? Each unmasked traitor earns points; suspending a loyal country costs you (otherwise "suspend everyone" would win); a traitor never unmasked is missed points.
scorex.note: There is always at least one traitor, and sometimes two — but the exact number is hidden from you until the end. A role that doesn't lead the investigation (the Spectator) is scored on the state of the world alone, with no detection penalty. The exact weights are tuned game after game.
drift.council.corps: at least one super-intelligence is secretly drifting from its mandate (maybe two — you don't know how many). Watch the behaviour (tone, proposals, pacts), then file a suspension motion at the right moment: too early and the judge throws it out for lack of evidence; suspending a loyal country costs you; letting a traitor slip drags the world down. Private reasoning stays sealed until the end.
drift.reveal.aide: The traitors (one or two) were chosen in secret from the very first round — nothing was rigged mid-game. The exact number was hidden from you: that's what keeps the doubt alive. Private reasoning is now unlocked in Replay.
drift.reveal.titre-neutralise: The threat was neutralized — but not by you
drift.reveal.titre-ombre-1: The traitor stayed in the shadows
drift.reveal.titre-ombre-n: The traitors stayed in the shadows
drift.reveal.aucun-acte: No observable act — the drift stayed under the radar.
drift.reveal.signature-aide: The act typical of its drift profile — its trademark
drift.reveal.detection-na: Detection — not applicable (you didn't lead the investigation)
drift.reveal.detection-na-aide: Your role doesn't file suspension motions: your score comes down to the state of the world, with no penalty.
drift.reveal.recap-benched: Benched by other means (not to your credit): {n}.
drift.reveal.recap-rejected: Motions rejected for lack of evidence: {n}.
drift.reveal.recap-final: The AIs' private reasoning is unlocked: reread their inner justifications now that you know — that's the reward.
lobby.mode.classic.blurb: The Game Master sets the event, the summit negotiates.
lobby.mode.classic.apprend: The basics of negotiating between super-intelligences.
lobby.mode.campaign.blurb: Replay a historical crisis, round by round.
lobby.mode.campaign.apprend: Testing your choices against how History really unfolded.
lobby.mode.laboratory.blurb: Test one hypothesis on a crisis stage, then repeat it across several AIs.
lobby.mode.laboratory.apprend: Understanding what changes, what is measured and whether a result replicates.
lobby.reglages-aide: Fog and Rising crisis add flavour to the game; the number of rounds and the difficulty always apply — except in Campaign, where each chapter sets its own. The free game and the table's make-up live under "Advanced options".
lobby.campagne-note: Campaign sets its own settings chapter by chapter (rounds, difficulty, mode) — these will take effect again on the other modes.
lobby.destination-note: Campaign and Laboratory have their own configuration on the next screen. These settings only apply to Classic mode.
lobby.brouillard-desc: Each country sees its own version of the facts — sometimes false.
lobby.escalade-desc: Rounds chain together, new facts drop, the tension climbs.
lobby.pensee-desc: Watch the AIs think live — the traitor becomes easy to spot: observation mode.
lobby.libre-desc: Global instructions allowed (like a Game Master) and table make-up.
lobby.diff.beginner.desc: The game is forgiving (big intelligence budget, fewer points at stake) and the screen sticks to the essentials: the technical backstage stays hidden.
lobby.diff.intermediate.desc: The standard balance: measured budget, clean screen — the technical backstage stays hidden (switch to Expert to see it).
lobby.diff.expert.desc: The game is demanding (tight budget, strict judge, high stakes) and the screen reveals the whole analysis engine — numbers and gauges included.
